name: CALM 1.2 Relationship Types Reference
description: A reference architecture demonstrating all 5 CALM 1.2 relationship types using an FSI payments platform scenario. Each relationship illustrates a distinct relationship-type: interacts, connects (with HTTPS), connects (with JDBC), deployed-in, composed-of, and options. The protocol field on connects relationships is always at the TOP LEVEL of the relationship object — never nested inside the connects sub-object.

nodes.name: Payments Analyst
nodes.description: Human user who initiates payment instructions and monitors settlement status via the payments portal.

nodes.name: Payments Portal
nodes.description: Browser-based web application providing the UI for submitting and tracking payment instructions.

nodes.name: Payments Service
nodes.description: Core backend service responsible for validating, routing, and processing payment instructions.

nodes.name: Notifications Service
nodes.description: Service that sends payment status notifications to downstream consumers via message queue.

nodes.name: Payments Database
nodes.description: PostgreSQL database storing payment instructions, routing decisions, and settlement confirmations.

nodes.name: AWS VPC (Production)
nodes.description: AWS Virtual Private Cloud forming the production runtime environment. Services and databases are deployed-in this ecosystem.

nodes.name: Payments Platform
nodes.description: The logical system boundary that structurally encompasses the Payments Service and Notifications Service as its composed parts.

relationships.description: Payments analyst accesses the payments portal to submit and track payment instructions. The actor field must reference a node of node-type actor.

Payments portal calls the Payments Service API over HTTPS to submit payment instructions and retrieve status. Note: protocol is at the top level of this relationship object, not inside the connects sub-object.
Payments Service reads and writes payment records to the Payments Database using JDBC. Protocol is at the top level — a sibling of relationship-type.
The Payments Service, Notifications Service, and Payments Database are deployed-in the AWS VPC. The container field references the ecosystem node that acts as the runtime environment.
The Payments Platform system is structurally composed-of the Payments Service and Notifications Service. This is a logical/structural boundary, not a runtime deployment relationship.

An architectural decision point: the Payments Service can notify downstream consumers either synchronously (via HTTPS callbacks) or asynchronously (via AMQP message queue). The options relationship captures this ADR-in-spec decision branch.

options.description: Synchronous notification: Payments Service calls downstream consumers directly over HTTPS. Simpler integration but tighter coupling and shared failure domain.
Asynchronous notification: Payments Service publishes to a message queue; Notifications Service consumes independently. Decoupled, resilient, but introduces a message broker dependency.
